items.code:
from django.contrib import admin
from django.urls import path
from . import views

urlpatterns = [
    path('admin/', admin.site.urls),
    path('api/public', views.public),
    path('api/private', views.private),
    path('api/private-scoped', views.private_scoped)
]

items.comment: Wrong highlight
items.code:
import './assets/main.css'

import { createApp } from 'vue'
import { createPinia } from 'pinia'
import router from './router'
import { createAuth0 } from '@auth0/auth0-vue'

import App from './App.vue'

const app = createApp(App)

app.use(createAuth0({
  domain: import.meta.env.VITE_AUTH0_DOMAIN,
  clientId: import.meta.env.VITE_AUTH0_CLIENT_ID,
  authorizationParams: {
    redirect_uri: window.location.origin
  }
}))

app.use(createPinia())
app.use(router)

app.mount('#app')

[] It mentions "The Ruby and PHP Management API SDKs are pending updates." without any timeline or channel for receiving updates on this matter. It's not convenient to offer a "migration guide" that cannot actually be followed if you're depending on pending SDK updates.

[] Looking at the documented text keys for the reset-password-mfa-email-challenge screen, I don't see a key for a "Code has been resent" confirmation message. The documented keys cover the resend action (resendActionText, resendText) but no success/confirmation message.  Based on the documentation, the key resendLimitReachedText is used for the message "Code has been resent." on other screens (like phone-identifier-challenge). However, this key is not documented for the reset-password-mfa-email-challenge screen in the customize-templates page.

items.code:
curl --request POST \
  --url 'https://{yourDomain}/oauth/token' \
  --header 'content-type: application/x-www-form-urlencoded' \
  --data grant_type=authorization_code \
  --data 'client_id={yourClientId}' \
  --data 'client_secret={yourClientSecret}' \
  --data 'code=yourAuthorizationCode}' \
  --data 'redirect_uri={https://yourApp/callback}'
